notes: Returns full table data including all fields, type settings, and grid views. Response may nest under a 'table' key.
Creates a new column. Type settings vary by column type. activeViewId required. 150ms delay recommended between calls.
Returns source details including sourceSubscriptions (which table/field the source feeds). state.numSourceRecords shows row count. No webhook URL visible on manual-type sources.
Creates a new data source. workspaceId is numeric. dataFieldId may not be immediately available.
Read table metadata. Documented in Clay reference but exact response shape not fully specified.
Read rows from a table. Pagination parameters need empirical verification.
Add rows to a table. Column references may be by name or ID.
Trigger enrichment runs. Exact parameters need verification.
Used by Lele credential system to validate Clay API keys. Returns sources list.
Public status endpoint. Returns current frontend version (format: vYYYYMMDD_HHMMSSz_hash) and CASL-style auth abilities. No auth required.
TABLE CREATION. Iterative Zod validation confirmed required fields. Auth 401 when payload is valid.
Table rename/update. Returns full table object with fields, views, abilities. Rename is instant. Tested: rename + revert both succeeded.
Table deletion. Returns 401 with valid table ID format.

NOT AN ENDPOINT. 'recent' is treated as a table ID. Returns 404 'Table recent does not exist'. INV-006 reported 401 (unauthenticated) but that was the table lookup auth check. Only /v3/workspaces/{id}/tables works for listing.
NOT AN ENDPOINT. Same false positive as /v3/tables/recent.
NOT AN ENDPOINT. Same false positive as /v3/tables/recent.
NOT AN ENDPOINT. Same false positive as /v3/tables/recent.
Workspace details including billing, credits, feature flags. Regular users can access their own workspace. GET /v3/workspaces (list all) requires admin.

response_shape.results: Table[] — each with id, workspaceId, name, description, type, icon, tableSettings, createdAt, updatedAt, workbookId, abilities

notes: List all tables in workspace. Returns {results: [...]}. Confirmed 11 tables returned.
Workspace creation. Returns 401 (exists).

response_shape: unknown (endpoint returns validation errors for bad payloads but success response shape not captured yet)
notes: Trigger enrichment/action runs on specific rows and fields. IMPORTANT: runRecords is an OBJECT not a string. Zod says: fieldIds=array(required), runRecords=object(required). Use {recordIds: [...]} or similar structure.
Column/field update. Returns 401 (exists). PUT returns 404 — use PATCH only.
Column/field deletion. Returns 401 (exists).
Source update. Empty body = no-op returns current state. Tested successfully.
Source deletion. Returns 401 (exists).

response_shape: Source[] — each with id, workspaceId, name, type, typeSettings, state, recordSourceUpdatedAt, sourceSubscriptions
notes: List all sources in workspace via GET /v3/sources?workspaceId=. Returns array directly. NOTE: /v3/sources/list returns 404 — use query param on /v3/sources instead.
Current user info. Returns API token, auth strategy, workspace IDs. Also refreshes session cookie via set-cookie header (7-day expiry resets on every call).

response_shape.actions: Action[] — each with key, version, package (id, key, displayName, icon, categories), displayName, description, inputParameterSchema, outputParameterSchema, auth ({providerType}), rateLimitRules, actionLabels, iconUri, actionEnablementInfo

notes: Returns ALL 1191 available enrichment actions with full I/O schemas, rate limits, provider types. Auth block only has providerType — no authAccountId (that's per-column). 170+ unique providers.
Create action package. actionPackageDefinition must be a string, not object. Payload structure confirmed via validation errors.

response_shape: Import[] — each with id, workspaceId, createdAt, updatedAt, finishedAt, createdBy, config (column mapping), source references
notes: Import history. Returns array of import records with config and column mapping details. 26 records returned for test workspace.

Creates import job. Executes SYNCHRONOUSLY — state.status=FINISHED typically before response returns. Requires the CSV already uploaded to S3 under the given key; no v3 upload/presign endpoint exists (upload must happen outside v3 — Clay UI uses a separate flow). INV-020 verified: reusing an existing key from import history imports 49 rows into the destination table in <1s. Confirmed 2026-04-07.
Get import job by ID. Supports polling for import state. Confirmed INV-020 2026-04-07.
NOT A SEPARATE ENDPOINT. 'csv' is treated as an import job ID. Returns 404 'Import Job with id csv not found'. Pattern is /v3/imports/{jobId}.
NOT A SEPARATE ENDPOINT. Same as /v3/imports/csv — 'webhook' treated as job ID.
CSV export endpoint. Returns 401 (exists).
Export download endpoint. Returns 401 (exists).
User listing. Requires admin session auth.
Current user details. Requires admin session auth. Used by Lele preflight for credential validation.
Source deletion. Returns {success: true}. Clean deletion observed. No cascading errors.

response_shape: AppAccount[] — each with id (authAccountId), name, appAccountTypeId (provider), isSharedPublicKey, userOwnerId, workspaceOwnerId, abilities, obfuscatedCredentials, useStaticIP
notes: BREAKTHROUGH: Lists ALL auth accounts (Clay-managed + user-owned). The 'id' field IS the authAccountId needed for enrichment columns. Also accessible via GET /v3/workspaces/{id}/app-accounts. 111 accounts returned for test workspace. POST/GET-by-ID both 404 — read-only.
List all workspaces. Requires admin auth. Regular users get 403: 'You must be logged in as an admin'. Use GET /v3/workspaces/{id} for specific workspace.

response_shape.records: Record[] — each with id (r_xxx), tableId, cells (fieldId → {value}), recordMetadata, createdAt, updatedAt

notes: ROW CREATION via v3. Cells keys must be field IDs (f_xxx), values are plain strings/numbers. Multiple records per call. Replaces deprecated v1 POST /api/v1/tables/{id}/rows.
ROW UPDATE via v3. Updates are async (enqueued). Replaces deprecated v1 API.
ROW DELETION via v3. Pass array of record IDs. Returns empty object on success.

request_shape.limit: number (query param, optional — limits result count)
request_shape.offset: number (query param, accepted but IGNORED — does not change starting position)

response_shape.results: Record[] — each with id (r_xxx), tableId, cells ({fieldId: {value, metadata}}), recordMetadata ({runHistory, preprocessingMarkerMax}), createdAt, updatedAt, deletedBy, dedupeValue

notes: ROW READING via v3. BREAKTHROUGH: Reading rows requires a view ID — there is no view-less GET endpoint. View IDs (gv_xxx) come from GET /v3/tables/{tableId} response under table.views[]. Views apply server-side filtering (e.g. 'Fully enriched rows' returns only enriched records, 'All rows' returns everything). limit param works. offset param is accepted but ignored (always returns from start). No pagination metadata in response — no hasMore, total, nextCursor. For full table reads, use the 'All rows' or 'Default view' view. Confirmed on multiple tables.

SINGLE ROW READ via v3. Fetch one record by ID. Returns the full record object (same shape as items in the view-based list endpoint). Returns 404 'Record {id} was not found' for invalid IDs. NOTE: The route pattern /v3/tables/{id}/records/{recordId} means any sub-path like /records/count or /records/query is interpreted as a record ID lookup, not a sub-resource.
DEPRECATED. api.clay.com returns Express HTML 404. api.clay.run returns {success:false,message:'deprecated API endpoint'}. All v1 endpoints are non-functional as of 2026-04-06.
DEPRECATED. See GET /api/v1/tables/{tableId}/rows note. Use POST /v3/tables/{id}/records instead.
DEPRECATED. Use PATCH /v3/tables/{id}/run instead.

response_shape.table: full Table object with id, fields, views, workbookId

notes: TABLE DUPLICATION. Creates a copy of the table with all columns. Name defaults to 'Copy of {original}'. Returns full table object. Also works via POST /v3/tables with sourceTableId or duplicateFromTableId params.
VIEW CREATION. Creates a new view on a table. Returns full view object. Only this path works — /v3/views and /v3/grid-views both 404.

VIEW UPDATE. Rename confirmed working. Filter/sort PATCH returns 200 but values show null in response — payload format may need refinement (accepted but possibly not persisted). Only /v3/tables/{id}/views/{viewId} path works.
WORKBOOK DUPLICATION. Copies entire workbook. Name defaults to 'Copy of {original}'.
WORKBOOK CREATION. Creates a new empty workbook. GET /v3/workbooks/{id} and PATCH /v3/workbooks/{id} both 404 — individual workbook read/update not available.

CSV EXPORT JOB CREATION. Async job model: POST to create job → poll GET /v3/exports/{jobId} for uploadedFilePath → download. The GET /v3/exports/csv endpoint treats 'csv' as a job ID (404 'Export job csv not found').

request_shape.resourceId: string (query, required — numeric user id as string)

notes: List user API keys. resourceType enum (Gb) has ONLY 'user' — workspace-scoped list returns 400. Verified end-to-end INV-028.

response_shape: ApiKey & { apiKey: string (plaintext, returned ONCE, 'ak_'-prefixed for id)}
notes: Mint an API key for the current user. Plaintext apiKey is in the response ONCE — same pattern as Clay's UI modal ('will not be displayed again'). UI exposes only 3 of 7 scopes in checkboxes (all, endpoints:prospect-search-api, public-endpoints:all); the terracotta:* scopes are mintable only via direct API. Verified INV-028 with 5 scratch keys, all deleted afterwards. Credit cost: zero.
Rename or re-scope an API key. Not exercised in INV-028 (delete+recreate worked). Bundle-confirmed in TRe router.
Revoke an API key. Verified end-to-end INV-028 — deleted 5 scratch keys cleanly.

response_shape.signals: Signal[] — each with id (sig_xxx), workspaceId, type, settings (version, signalType, monitorType, actionPackageId, actionTaskResumeBehavior)

notes: SIGNAL MONITORING. Lists all signals configured for the workspace. Signals monitor data sources and trigger actions. Rich settings object with action package references.
RESOURCE TAGS. Lists all tags in workspace. Currently returns empty array. POST/DELETE untested.

response_shape.users: User[] — each with id, username, email, name, profilePicture, fullName, role ({id, name})

notes: WORKSPACE USERS. Lists all members of the workspace with their roles and profile info. GET /v3/users requires admin; this workspace-scoped version works for regular users.

response_shape.userPermissions: UserPermission[] — each with roleId, email, pending (boolean), user object

notes: WORKSPACE PERMISSIONS. Lists role-based permissions per user. Shows role assignments and pending invitations.

attributeDescriptionsMap.waterfallAttributes: Map of attribute enum → {enum, entityType (person|company), displayName, icon, dataTypeSettings, isPopular, actionIds}

notes: ATTRIBUTE CATALOG. Returns Clay's full enrichment attribute taxonomy. Person attributes (workEmail, fullName, linkedinUrl), company attributes (domain, name). Each attribute maps to specific enrichment actions via actionIds.

EXPORT JOB STATUS. Poll this after POST /v3/tables/{id}/export. Status transitions: ACTIVE → FINISHED. uploadedFilePath becomes populated when job completes (e.g., 'ws1080480/filename.csv'). First poll after 2s returned FINISHED. Download path is relative — actual download mechanism TBD.
VIEW DELETION. Returns empty object on success. Cannot delete all views — at least one must remain.
PRESETS. Endpoint exists but requires admin auth. 403 'You must be logged in as an admin'. Regular workspace users cannot access.

response_shape.signal: Signal object with id, workspaceId, type, settings (signalType, monitorType, actionPackageId, actionKey, actionInputBindings)

notes: SINGLE SIGNAL READ. Returns one signal by ID. Write operations (POST/PATCH/DELETE) return 404 — signals can only be read, not managed via API.
TAG CREATION. Creates a resource tag in the workspace. Color must be one of 8 enum values. Tag ID format: tag_xxx.
TAG DELETION. Deletes a resource tag by ID.

FIELD UPDATE. Rename confirmed working. Formula text update confirmed. Cannot change field type (text→formula returns 400). Must include full typeSettings when updating (formulaText + formulaType + dataTypeSettings).

response_shape: Full table object with updated workbookId
notes: TABLE WORKBOOK RE-PARENTING. PATCH table with {workbookId} successfully moves the table to a different workbook. parentFolderId also accepted as a concept (409 'folder not found' when invalid, not 404).

response_shape.uploadUrls: Array<{url: string (presigned S3 PUT URL), partNumber: number}>

notes: INITIATE MULTIPART CSV UPLOAD. Returns a list of presigned S3 PUT URLs (one per 50MB part). Caller PUTs each part directly to S3 with Content-Type: application/octet-stream, captures Etag headers, then calls /complete. Single-part for files <50MB. Used by Clay UI's CSVFileUpload component. With toS3CSVImportBucket:true the resulting key is consumable by POST /v3/imports.

request_shape.s3key: string (LOWERCASE k — note inconsistency with init response 's3Key')
request_shape.etags: Array<{partNumber: number, etag: string (UNWRAPPED — strip surrounding quotes from S3 ETag header)}>

notes: COMPLETE MULTIPART CSV UPLOAD. Finalizes the S3 multipart upload after all parts have been PUT to their presigned URLs. After this completes, the s3Key from the init call can be passed as source.key in POST /v3/imports.

response_shape.fields: {bucket, key, Policy, X-Amz-Algorithm, X-Amz-Credential, X-Amz-Date, X-Amz-Security-Token, X-Amz-Signature}

notes: WORKFLOWS CSV UPLOAD (S3 POST policy variant). Exercised end-to-end INV-023: POST init returns S3 POST policy, caller does multipart/form-data POST (fields first, then `file` last) directly to S3 which returns 204. The uploadToken is then passed to the workflow-run-batch create endpoint. Targets the SAME clay-base-import-prod bucket as multi-part-upload but via POST policy instead of PUT. 400 with missing fields returns structured BadRequest{type,message,details}.

response_shape.fields: {bucket, key, Policy, X-Amz-Algorithm, X-Amz-Credential, X-Amz-Date, X-Amz-Security-Token, X-Amz-Signature}

notes: DOCUMENTS UPLOAD (S3 POST policy variant). Exercised end-to-end INV-023. Caller POSTs init → gets documentId + S3 POST policy → does multipart/form-data POST to file-drop-prod (fields first, file last) → S3 returns 204 → caller POSTs /v3/documents/{ws}/{documentId}/confirm-upload (empty body) → returns full document record. Targets file-drop-prod bucket (same as toS3CSVImportBucket:false on multi-part-upload). Default context is 'agent_playground'.

CONFIRM DOCUMENT UPLOAD. Called after a successful S3 POST to the upload-url presigned endpoint. Makes the document visible/queryable in the Documents feature. Discovered+verified alongside /v3/documents/{ws}/upload-url during INV-023.

response_shape.workflows: Array<{id, workspaceId, creatorUserId, name, defaultModelId, lastRunAt, createdAt, updatedAt}>

notes: LIST WORKFLOWS for a workspace. Tanstack Workflows (tc-workflows) team. Empty array for workspaces with no workflows. Verified INV-023.

response_shape.workflow: {id (wf_...), workspaceId, creatorUserId, name, defaultModelId, lastRunAt, createdAt, updatedAt}

notes: CREATE WORKFLOW. tc-workflows team. Verified INV-023 (scratch workflow created + deleted).
DELETE WORKFLOW. Verified INV-023 during scratch workflow cleanup.
DELETE DOCUMENT. Supports query params ?hard=boolean and ?deleteContents=boolean. Verified INV-023 with hard=true during cleanup of test document.

request_shape: Discriminated union on `type`: csv_import {workflowSnapshotId: 'latest'|wfs_id, type:'csv_import', csvUploadToken: string (uuid from csv-upload-url), config?: object (e.g. {standaloneActions: []})} OR cpj_search {workflowSnapshotId, type:'cpj_search', config?: object}

response_shape.batch: {id (wfrb_...), workflowSnapshotId (wfs_...), workflowId, workflowName, status: 'pending'|'running'|'completed'|'failed'|'cancelled', type: 'csv_import'|'cpj_search', createdBy, config, state: {lastOffsetProcessed: number}, createdAt, updatedAt, totalRuns, completedRuns, failedRuns, pendingRuns, runningRuns}

notes: CREATE WORKFLOW RUN BATCH (createWorkflowRunBatch). Closes the tc-workflows ingestion loop after csv-upload-url + S3 POST. Backend snapshots the workflow (wfs_...), parses the CSV header into config.parameterNames, and either spawns runs OR transitions immediately to 'failed' if the workflow has zero defined steps. Verified end-to-end INV-024 with a scratch workflow + 1-row CSV — batch created at 200, no credits consumed (totalRuns=0). Empty body returns BadRequest with discriminator error.

request_shape: query: {limit?: number (default 50), offset?: number (default 0), status?: string}
notes: LIST WORKFLOW RUN BATCHES. Verified INV-024.
GET WORKFLOW RUN BATCH by id. Used for polling status transitions. Verified INV-024.

UPDATE WORKFLOW RUN BATCH. Verified INV-025: PATCH {status:'cancelled'} on a pending csv_import batch returns 200 with batch.status='cancelled'. Must race the auto-fail (~430ms for empty workflows). Subsequent GET also returns 'cancelled'.
DELETE WORKFLOW RUN BATCH. Verified INV-024 during cleanup.
LIST RUNS FOR A BATCH. Empty {runs:[], total:0} when the batch failed before spawning any runs (workflow had zero steps). Verified INV-024.
Returns full graph for a tc-workflow plus server-side validation (isValid + per-node errors/warnings). Validation surfaces missing model/prompt/output schema requirements.

Creates a workflow node. 'regular' nodes with no model/prompt are inert (zero credits). Inert nodes are safe scratch resources. Top-level enum SCe in bundle is ['regular','code','conditional','map','reduce','tool'].
Update a workflow node. Verified renaming + description patch returns 200 with updated node. Many additional fields available (model, prompt source, tool ids, inline scripts).
Batch update multiple node positions in one call. Useful for graph layout autosave.
Delete a single workflow node.
Batch delete multiple workflow nodes by id.
Duplicate a node along with its edges. Bundle-extracted, not exercised in INV-025.
Download the Python source for a 'code' node. Bundle-extracted, not exercised.
Create an edge between two workflow nodes. Verified end-to-end.
Update edge metadata (e.g. handoff config for agent edges). Bundle-extracted, not exercised.
Delete a workflow edge. Verified.

List workflow snapshots. Snapshots are AUTO-CREATED by createWorkflowRunBatch when workflowSnapshotId='latest'. Returns empty array until first batch is created. content embeds the full workflow definition at snapshot time.
Get a single snapshot by id (wfs_...). Verified end-to-end with auto-created snapshot from a csv_import batch.
Create a brand new workflow from an existing snapshot. Bundle-extracted, not exercised.
Restore an existing workflow to a previous snapshot's content. Bundle-extracted, not exercised.
Create a workflow from a Clay-curated preset. Preset id source unknown.
Duplicate an existing tc-workflow.
Update workflow top-level metadata. Confirmed via bundle (uYe router); not directly probed in INV-025 but the create/delete pair was.

request_shape.standaloneActions: object? (NOT an array — server rejects array with 400)

notes: Direct workflow run (Swe router — INV-025 name 'Ewe' was a guess). Starts executing IMMEDIATELY and auto-resolves 'latest' workflow snapshot. Verified INV-026 against inert regular nodes — zero workspace credit cost (Clay's internal Claude Haiku LLM calls are NOT metered to caller workspace). Accepted shapes: {}, {inputs:{}}, {inputs:{any}}. Rejected: {standaloneActions:[]} (expects object).
List runs for a workflow. Query params: limit (default 50), offset (default 0). Verified INV-026. runState inlined per status.

Get a workflow run with all its steps. Returns archived form if the run has been archived to cold storage. Verified INV-026 — completed run returns type:'current' with full step telemetry incl. token usage, system/user prompts, thread context.

Pause an active workflow run. 400 'Cannot pause workflow run with status X' when already in a terminal state (completed|failed|cancelled). Verified rejection path INV-026 — happy path requires a long-running workflow to catch it mid-flight.
Resume a paused workflow run. 400 'Cannot unpause workflow run with status X' when not paused. Verified rejection path INV-026.

request_shape.humanFeedbackInput: discriminated union on 'type': ApproveToolCall{toolName,approveToolCallForEntireRun} | RejectToolCall | DenyToolCall{preventFutureToolCallsWithThisTool,toolName,feedback?} | DenyTransition{targetNodeId,feedback?} | ... (hCe in bundle)

notes: Human-in-the-loop: continue a waiting workflow run step by providing feedback. Verified 404 'Workflow run step not found' for fake stepId INV-026; endpoint exists. Happy path requires a step in 'waiting' state (use GET /steps/waiting to find candidates).

List all workflow run steps currently waiting for human input within a workflow. Used to drive the human-in-the-loop UI. Verified INV-026 returns [] when no steps are waiting. callbackData is a discriminated union covering human_input_tool_decision, human_input_transition_decision, async_tool_execution, max_uninterrupted_steps_reached, workflow_run_paused, wait_tool_execution, code_execution_pending/complete, tool_node_execution_pending/complete.

Create a tc-workflow run stream (lKe router — INV-025/INV-026 guess 'sKe' was the request body schema, not the router). For streamType='webhook' the response includes a webhookUrl pointing at https://api.clay.com/tc-workflows/streams/{id}/webhook (no /v3 in returned URL but the /v3-prefixed path is what's actually routable under session cookie). Verified end-to-end INV-027.
List all streams on a workflow. Query: limit (default 50), offset (default 0), status, streamType. Verified INV-027.
Get a single workflow run stream. Verified INV-027.
Update a workflow run stream. Verified INV-027 toggling status to 'paused'. Once paused, postWebhook returns 400 'Stream is not active'.
Soft-delete a workflow run stream (sets deletedAt; runs spawned by the stream remain). Verified INV-027.
List runs spawned by a particular stream. Same Q_ WorkflowRun shape as direct/batch runs. Query: limit, offset, status (pending|running|paused|completed|failed). Verified INV-027.

request_shape: Record<string,any> — arbitrary JSON, becomes runState.inputs verbatim
notes: Live webhook ingestion (uKe router). Root path: NO /workspaces/{ws} prefix — streamId is globally unique. 202 Accepted with synchronous workflowRunId. Errors: 400 BadRequest (Stream is not active), 404 NotFound (Stream not found), 429 with retryAfter. Body becomes the new run's runState.inputs unmodified. INV-027 verified under session cookie; **INV-028 proved the endpoint is COMPLETELY UNAUTHENTICATED** — POST with zero auth headers returns 202 and creates a run. streamId is the bearer token (same security model as table webhooks or Slack incoming webhooks). Any auth header present is accepted/ignored. This is Clay's productized inbound channel for tc-workflows. **Bug**: Clay returns webhookUrl='https://api.clay.com/tc-workflows/streams/{id}/webhook' (without /v3) in the stream create response, but that URL form 404s — consumers must rewrite to prepend /v3.

Batch webhook ingestion (uKe router). Bundle-confirmed; reachable but returns 401 under every user-facing auth scheme tested in INV-028 (Bearer, x-api-key, x-clay-api-key, X-Api-Key, Clay-API-Key, query-param apiKey, Basic, Token, x-auth-token, plus no-auth) across 4 different scope sets (terracotta:cli, terracotta:*, all, union). Returns 403 under session cookies (with canonical body) or Clay-API-Key header. Zod validation runs before auth (wrong bodies return 400 with 'items Required'), proving the route handler is reached. No frontend caller exists for this route in the bundle — contrast with postWebhook (single) which is directly called from the Workflows editor. Conclusion: this is an **internal-only route** used by Clay's own async workers for backfill ingestion, NOT a productized API-key endpoint. GAP-036 closed by elimination in INV-028 — the productized inbound channel is the single-event postWebhook route (unauthenticated).
